data.title: Nvidia Stock (NVDA) Looks Less Tasty Despite Taking AI to Noodles Centre of Japan
data.description:
Shares in semiconductor giant Nvidia (NVDA) slipped today despite striking a breakthrough AI deal in a Japanese region best known for its tasty noodles.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

First Regional AI Deal

Nvidia said that it had signed a partnership with Kagawa Prefecture in Western Japan to expand the use of AI among companies in the region and look into the development of data centers. The partnership is the first such deal struck by a local government in Japan.

Kagawa Prefecture, Japan's smallest by area and well known for udon noodles, has been promoting the region as an ideal place to work, particularly toward technology companies, due to its low risk for natural disasters and stable electricity supply.

It also hopes to improve the productivity of firms located within the prefecture by gathering companies utilizing graphics processing units developed by Nvidia, the prefectural government said.

"We take pride in Kagawa's advantageous location when it comes to the operation of GPU centers," Kagawa Gov. Toyohito Ikeda said. "We hope to expand the implementation of AI for firms in the prefecture by enlisting Nvidia's help."

Masataka Osaki, head of Nvidia's Japanese unit, said, "A range of AI catered to individual regions has been launched globally. We hope Kagawa Prefecture will become a role model across the country."

Nvidia's Global Expansion

As seen below, Nvidia's revenues are driven by the U.S. but it is trying to expand its global reach. It's already been making progress in Japan.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Tesla Stock Gains a Tailwind as Elon Musk Confirms "No Pedals or Steering Wheel" Cybercab Production Starts in April
data.description:
Tesla stock (TSLA) could be gearing up for a key tailwind following a major update from its CEO Elon Musk. On Monday, Musk took to X to repeat his aggressive timeline for the company's autonomous future. Quoting an old clip where an audience laughed at his prediction that cars would one day lose their steering wheels, Musk posted: "Cybercab, which has no pedals or steering wheel, starts production in April". This confirmation comes as the project moves into its final manufacturing phase, with the first units expected to roll off the line in less than two months.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Musks Wants to Build Vehicles that are 5 Times Faster than Before

The Cybercab represents a complete departure from the way Tesla usually makes cars. Musk explained that the company is moving toward a radical new assembly method to eventually build vehicles five times faster than before. While he expects the final production volume to be very high, he warned that the early stages in April will be "agonizingly slow" as the team gets the new system up and running. Even with rumors about safety rules possibly forcing a change, Musk is sticking to the plan that the final car will have no pedals or wheel at all. The long-term goal is to have a finished Cybercab leave the factory every 10 seconds.

Tesla Stock Reacts

Investors are currently weighing this ai">ambitious timeline against Tesla's history of meeting tough deadlines. As of Tuesday morning, Tesla stock is trading around $412.20, down about 1.26% in premarket trading. While the stock has grown about 17.5% over the last year, it recently hit a 10-week low, showing that some people are still unsure if Tesla can pull off this April goal without traditional controls. Some experts think the money from a future robotaxi software network could be a massive win, but the lack of clear legal approval for a car without a steering wheel remains a major risk for shareholders.

Musk's 10-year Prediction Comes True

The tweet that sparked this latest talk featured a 10-year-old video of Musk being mocked for his vision. Today, that vision is turning into something real, with prototypes already showing up in stores featuring road-legal lights and updated parts. By skipping the step where a human safety driver sits behind the wheel, Tesla is betting everything on the idea that its AI software can handle the road on its own from day one. If this works, the Cybercab could transform Tesla from a standard car maker into a leading self-driving taxi giant.

Is Tesla Stock a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

On Wall Street, analysts have maintained a cautious stance on Tesla stock. According to TipRanks, TSLA stock has received a Hold consensus rating, with 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month price target for Tesla stock is $396.80, suggesting a downside of 5% from the current level.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/17/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Dip in Holiday-Shortened Week Amid Key Data
data.description:
U.S. stock futures opened lower Tuesday, kicking off a holiday-shortened trading week in the red. Traders await key employment figures, FOMC minutes, and delayed economic reports.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.56%, 0.20%, and 0.02%, respectively, at 5:30 a.m. EST on February 17.

Last week, all three major indexes closed lower. The S&P 500 and Dow Jones each dropped more than 1%, while the Nasdaq Composite fell over 2%, marking its fifth straight week of losses. Ongoing concerns about AI disrupting software, real estate, and financial services weighed on investor sentiment.

Turning to economic data, delayed reports on the NAHB housing data for November and December, along with the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index for December (the Fed's preferred inflation gauge), will be released this week. Additionally, data on retail sales, FOMC Minutes, ADP Employment report, and initial jobless claims are due this week.

On the earnings front, results from Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Medtronic (MDT), Booking Holdings (BKNG), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), Walmart (WMT), Alibaba (BABA), Li Auto (LI), and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) will be reported this week.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.02%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $63.27 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $4,928 per ounce on Tuesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes rose on strong corporate earnings, despite the UK's unemployment hitting a five-year high of 5.2% and slowing growth. Traders are expecting at least two interest rate cuts from the Bank of England this year.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on February 17, with Mainland Chinese and Hong Kong markets remaining closed for the Lunar New Year.

Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei fell 0.42%, and the Topix decreased 0.68%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Disney Serves Legal Notice Demanding ByteDance to Halt AI-Generated Videos
data.description:
Disney ($DIS) has issued a cease-and-desist letter to the Beijing-based internet technology company ByteDance regarding its Seedance 2.0 generative AI video tool. The entertainment conglomerate claimed that Seedance 2.0 used Disney-owned intellectual property without authorization. ByteDance said it is taking steps to prevent misuse of copyrighted material on the platform.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Disney Accuses ByteDance of Copyright Violations

On Feb. 13, Disney sent a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance over its Seedance 2.0 AI model. The entertainment and media company alleged that the Chinese tech company used Disney-owned characters without permission and included a "pirated library" of content from franchises such as Star Wars and Marvel. It also stated that the characters are presented as if they were public domain material.

The letter also stated that Bytedance's Seedance 2.0 replicates, shares, and modifies these characters to generate new content. Consequently, Disney warned that this activity could have lasting effects and has drawn concern across the industry, with Paramount Skydance (PSKY) also issuing cease-and-desist letters over similar issues.

Disney's attorney, David Singer of Jenner & Block LLC, emphasized that the company views ByteDance's actions as infringing on intellectual property and creating a potential precedent for further misuse. The letter describes the alleged infringement as ongoing. 

ByteDance Responds as Industry Groups Condemn Tool

In response to Disney's legal notice, ByteDance said it respects intellectual property rights and is taking steps to prevent unauthorized use of copyrighted material and likenesses on Seedance 2.0. Released in February 2026 and currently available only in China, the tool has produced viral videos featuring actors such as Kim Kardashian, Tom Cruise, Brad Pitt, and Will Smith, and has reportedly used assets from franchises such as The Lord of the Rings. The company has not detailed the measures it plans to implement.

Industry groups have criticized the platform for potential copyright violations. The Motion Picture Association (MPA) said Seedance 2.0 engaged in unauthorized use of U.S. copyrighted works on a large scale. SAG-AFTRA, the actors' union, also condemned the tool, noting it allows the use of members' voices and likenesses without consent, which could affect real human talent.

Notably, Disney has previously taken action against various AI platforms. The entertainment company previously sent cease-and-desist letters to firms such as Character.AI and signed a licensing agreement with American artificial intelligence company OpenAI in Dec. 2025, allowing characters from Star Wars, Pixar, and Marvel to be used in OpenAI's Sora video generator.

Is Disney a Good Stock to Buy Now?

According to TipRanks, Disney is a "Strong Buy" based on analysts' ratings. To find out more about its price targets, financials, and analysts' forecasts, visit TipRanks Comparison Stock Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Logan Paul Sells Rare Pokémon Card for $16.5M amid Past NFT Lawsuit Drama
data.description:
YouTube star Logan Paul is back in the record books after selling his one-of-a-kind Pokémon card for nearly $16.5 million on Monday. The sale, which happened at Goldin Auctions, is now the most expensive trading card transaction in history. The buyer was AJ Scaramucci, the son of well-known financier Anthony Scaramucci. Paul originally bought the card for $5.3 million in 2021, meaning he walked away with a massive profit of about $8 million after auction fees.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Paul Faces Backlash over Digital Shares

While the $16.5 million price tag is shocking, it has also brought back old complaints about Paul's past crypto projects. In 2022, Paul tried to "fractionalize" the same card on a platform called Liquid Marketplace. This meant he sold tiny digital pieces of the card to fans so they could say they co-owned it. However, the website eventually went offline, and investors were left wondering what happened to their money.

Gabriel Shapiro, a legal expert at Delphi Labs, recently called the situation a "classic case of 'slop tokenization.'" He noted that "The token is basically just 'juxtaposed' with property but has no rights to it," and warned people to stay away from what he called "legal scams." Paul defended himself on Monday, claiming the site went down for reasons he couldn't control and that he eventually paid to fix it so users could get their money back.

Regulators Investigate Liquid Marketplace

Even though Paul made a record sale this week, the fallout from Liquid Marketplace is still active. The Ontario Securities Commission (OSC) in Canada brought a lawsuit against the platform in June 2024. While Logan Paul is not named personally in that specific lawsuit, the regulator is looking into how the platform handled investor funds. A hearing for that case is scheduled for this coming June, 2026.

This is not the first time Paul has faced scrutiny for NFT projects. His CryptoZoo game also failed to launch, leading to a different class-action lawsuit. In that case, Paul eventually set up a buyback program to pay back investors who agreed not to sue him further. This specific fraud lawsuit was dismissed in 2025.

Investors Exit the NFT Market

The huge price of Paul's physical card stands in stark contrast to the digital collectibles market. While the Pikachu Illustrator card reached a record high, the overall NFT market cap has crashed by more than 50% since the start of 2026. Data from CoinGecko shows the market fell from $3.2 billion to just $1.55 billion in a matter of weeks. High-profile marketplaces like Rodeo and Nifty Gateway have even started closing down their operations, signaling a very tough time for digital assets.

Key Takeaway

Essentially, Logan Paul just pulled off one of the biggest flips in history. He took a piece of cardboard he bought for $5.3 million and turned it into $16.5 million in just a few years. But while the physical card is worth more than ever, his reputation in the crypto world is still recovering. Many people are still upset about his digital projects that didn't work out. This tells us that while you can sell a record-breaking card, you can't always sell a clean history.

Investors can track the prices of well-established cryptos on the TipRanks Cryptocurrency Center. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: 'The Impunity of the Giants Must End': Meta Stock Slides as Spain Launches AI Probe
data.description:
Meta's (META) shares edged lower during Tuesday's pre-market session after Spain ordered a probe into the U.S. tech giant alongside X and TikTok. The government has alleged that the social media companies enabled the creation and distribution of child sexual abuse material generated with artificial intelligence on their platforms.

data.title: Shares of Indian Tech Giant Infosys (INFY) Soar on AI Collaboration with Anthropic
data.description:
Infosys Limited (INFY) stock rose more than 3% in Tuesday's pre-market trading after the Indian information technology (IT) giant announced a strategic collaboration with Anthropic to develop and deliver advanced enterprise AI (artificial intelligence) solutions to companies in the telecommunications, financial services, manufacturing, and software development markets. Infosys' India-listed shares (IN:INFY) gained about 2%.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Infosys Inks AI Deal with Anthropic

Infosys said that the collaboration will begin in telecommunications and then expand to other markets. Notably, a dedicated Anthropic Center of Excellence will be established to build and deploy AI agents customized for industry-specific operations.

Anthropic's Claude models will be integrated into Infosys' Topaz AI offerings to help enterprises automate complex workflows and accelerate software delivery. Infosys stated that AI adoption will be guided by the governance and transparency required by regulated industries.

Infosys highlighted that the focus of the collaboration with Anthropic will be agentic AI. The two companies will build systems that go beyond answering questions to independently handle multi-step tasks such as processing claims, generating and testing code, or managing compliance reviews.

India is considered a key market for AI adoption. The Asian country is currently hosting the Artificial Intelligence (AI) Impact Summit, with global tech leaders such as Alphabet (GOOGL) CEO Sundar Pichai and OpenAI's Sam Altman expected to attend the event.

Is Infosys Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on the U.S.-listed shares of Infosys. The average INFY stock price target of $19.72 indicates about 34% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: 96% of Quantum Funding Is Concentrated in 45 Global Hubs. Here's What It Means for Quantum Stocks
data.description:
Quantum computing is not spreading across the globe. Instead, it is flowing into a short list of tight hubs. This is according to a new study from the European Centre for International Political Economy. The study finds that 96% of global quantum company funding now goes to just 45 dense "quantum clusters." That is up from 92% only two years ago. In other words, more money is going to fewer places.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The report says the future of quantum "will not be decided country by country, but cluster by cluster." That line sums up the shift. Investors tracking the space should focus less on nations and more on cities.

To qualify as a full cluster, a region must host at least two startups with $10 million in combined funding, or one startup with $25 million or more in funding. It must also have at least five active research, industry, or state groups working in quantum. By that test, only 45 regions make the cut.
data.author: Ran Melamed
